title: Before landing
body: Before the plane lands, review your first three steps: confirm internet access, open your hotel information, and know how you plan to get to your hotel.

title: At the airport
body: As early as possible after landing, confirm that your phone and internet plan are working. If they are not, use airport Wi-Fi or ask at a service desk.

title: Keep your hotel details ready
body: Before leaving the airport, open your hotel booking, address, and phone number so you can show them quickly if needed.

title: Choose transport before you exit
body: Decide how you will get to your hotel before you leave the airport area. Making the decision earlier reduces confusion and stress.

title: Show your destination clearly
body: Keep your hotel or destination address visible on your phone so you can show it quickly during your first ride.

title: Keep backup payment ready
body: Do not rely on only one payment method during your first transfer. Keep one backup option available in case your main setup does not work.

title: Stabilize after check-in
body: After you check in, confirm your essentials: internet, hotel details, payment backup, and your plan for the next simple step.

title: Keep your first meal simple
body: Arrival day is not the best time for a complicated food decision. Choose an easy, low-risk first meal so you can settle in with less stress.

title: Use your food communication card
body: If you have dietary or religious restrictions, keep your food communication card ready before you order your first meal.

title: Keep your first local step light
body: If you go out again on the first day, do only one simple local step such as a short walk, a nearby meal, or a basic convenience stop.

title: Review tomorrow before sleeping
body: Before sleeping, check your main destination or plan for the next day so your second day starts with less uncertainty.
